source_jobs.title: AI Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE:

As AI Deployment Strategist, you’ll work across customer success, sales, and engineering to ensure technical clarity and execution—before, during, and after the sale of our cutting-edge F2 product. You’ll become the AI prompting authority for the business, forming the technical backbone of the go-to-market motion.




YOU WILL:

 - Support the F2 sales process by joining prospect and client calls to address technical product questions

 - Own customer due diligence: complete InfoSec questionnaires, and continuously improve our DDQ knowledge base

 - Audit reports generated by users, flag issues to engineering, and support customers in getting value from our AI

 - Prompt engineering: create natural language prompts tailored to customer needs to ensure accurate, relevant report output

 - Deliver light technical implementations, such as configuring SSO or provisioning custom data retention policies

 - Ensure CRM hygiene: maintain clean, structured data to help the customer success and sales teams scale effectively

 - Be the voice of the customer to the engineering team—providing real feedback that helps improve the product




WHO YOU ARE

You’re a versatile operator who can translate between engineers and customers. You know how to handle enterprise buyers, explain complex systems clearly, and work cross-functionally to deliver high-impact outcomes.

 - Experience prompting and working with LLMs and AI SaaS tools

 - Familiarity with enterprise information security concepts and due diligence processes

 - Strong communicator who is commercially minded, customer-obsessed, and tech fluent. You can explain complex concepts to non technical audiences

 - Ability to project manage complex workflows and technical deliverables without writing production code

 - Strong on keyboard skills, technically sharp, and eager to find a path to leveling up your coding skills

 - Based in NYC — we believe the best work is done together, in person at our beautiful corporate HQ in NYC. You’ll have flexibility to work remotely at least 1 day per week.




NICE TO HAVES

 - Prior experience reviewing or writing investment memos or working as an investment analyst is a big plus

 - Previous roles in technical sales, solutions engineering, customer success, or AI implementation

 - Familiarity with reporting tools, natural language outputs, or unstructured document processing

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT YOU'LL DO IN THE ROLE:

 - Build our Product. Own and implement software features that help to power our AI platform helping private markets investors analyze deals with velocity and accuracy. In this role you will be actively coding and delivering critical platform components from scratch.

 - Deliver excellence and quality. Set and uphold coding standards, architectural patterns, and review processes; mentor engineers. Co-own our culture of practical, reliable, and test-driven development that rapidly addresses business needs.

 - Build for Scale. Contribute to monitoring & scaling the architecture of the platform and the infrastructure.

 - Cross-functional collaboration. Actively partner with product, security, and go-to-market teams to translate our roadmap into features and deliverables.
   
   


WHAT YOU BRING TO THE ROLE:

 - 4+ years of fullstack software engineering experience.

 - Passion for engineering and learning.

 - AI fluency, and ability to build with AI at the core

 - Knowledgeable about modern web application development practices.

 - Familiarity with Python, Fast API, TypeScript, Node.js, Supabase, AWS is a plus.

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About F2

F2 is redefining how the financial sector operates by bridging the gap between legacy workflows in institutional finance and the future of AI-driven efficiency. While large financial firms comprise the backbone of the economy, their front and middle-office processes are still manual, spreadsheet-heavy, and fragmented.



F2 is the leading vertical AI platform purpose-built for private markets and commercial banking. We transform dense, unstructured deal materials into auditable, decision-ready insights in minutes. This is not generic AI tooling. It is core infrastructure embedded directly into underwriting, credit, and portfolio workflows. We are the only fully vertical platform in this space with meaningful, proven revenue and real product–market fit.



Role Overview

We are hiring an Enterprise Account Executive focused on commercial banking, investment banking, and private credit to own the full-cycle sales motion for F2’s AI platform within these institutions. You will run complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise sales cycles end to end, from first executive conversation through signed contract and expansion, while acting as a trusted advisor to senior banking leaders navigating AI adoption in regulated environments.



You will work closely with Product, Deployment, and Leadership to shape our enterprise go-to-market motion, bringing real-world institutional insight back into how we build, position, and scale F2 across private markets and commercial banking.



This role is ideal for a high-performing, domain-fluent operator with deep in-seat banking experience who thrives in ambiguity, understands how regulated institutions buy, and wants to help define how a leading vertical AI company wins true enterprise deals.



What You'll Do

 - Own the full-cycle sales process for F2's AI platform with commercial and investment banking clients—from first meeting through signed contract and initial expansion.

 - Prospect and engage Managing Directors, Group Heads, senior credit officers, and product leaders across corporate, commercial, and investment banking.

 - Collaborate with Solutions Engineers and Product to design workflows and demos that showcase F2's differentiated value in credit underwriting, monitoring, and deal workflows.

 - Lead pilots and proof-of-concepts, and coordinate with multiple stakeholders across front office, credit risk, operations, and technology.

 - Navigate complex legal, risk, compliance, and procurement processes typical of regulated financial institutions.

 - Build deep, multi-threaded relationships within key banking accounts, becoming a trusted advisor and long-term partner.

 - Capture customer feedback and market signals to inform F2's go-to-market strategy, pricing, and commercial banking product roadmap.

 - Contribute to playbooks and repeatable sales motions for commercial and investment banking as a core vertical.



You Might Be a Great Fit if You

 - Have deep, in-seat experience in investment banking, commercial banking, or private credit.

 - Bring a strong grasp of credit, underwriting, or capital markets workflows, or a clear aptitude to learn complex banking use cases quickly.

 - Have experience engaging senior banking leadership in high-stakes decision environments.

 - You are comfortable running long, strategic, multi-stakeholder enterprise cycles.

 - You thrive in high-ownership environments where domain credibility matters.

 - You want to operate at the intersection of finance and AI without leaving the institutional ecosystem behind.

 - You communicate with precision, credibility, and executive presence.

 - Bonus: Experience interfacing with risk, compliance, or technology teams within large financial institutions.



Why Join F2

 - Direct impact on a critical growth vertical as commercial banks, investment banks, and private credit institutions adopt AI to modernize underwriting and portfolio workflows.

 - Opportunity to sell a leading vertical AI platform

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative, Private Equity/Private Credit
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT F2

F2 is the AI platform for private markets investors — purpose-built to transform how institutional teams underwrite, diligence, and deploy capital. Our AI converts messy, unstructured deal materials into investment-grade insights in minutes, helping private credit funds, commercial banks, and private equity firms make faster, more confident decisions. Backed by leading investors, including NFX and Y Combinator, we’re scaling a world-class product and engineering team to build the future of vertical AI for finance.






ROLE OVERVIEW

As a Business Development Representative (BDR), you’ll be on the front lines of F2 AI’s go-to-market motion, focused on generating qualified pipeline within commercial banks. You’ll identify, engage, and qualify prospective customers, setting the foundation for successful enterprise sales cycles.

You’ll work closely with sales, marketing, and leadership to refine our outbound strategy and messaging, helping define how F2 AI breaks into large, regulated financial institutions.

This role is ideal for a driven, curious seller early in their career who wants deep exposure to enterprise sales, AI technology, and the commercial banking landscape.






WHAT YOU’LL DO

As a BDR at F2, your primary mission is pipeline creation. You’ll play a critical role in identifying and qualifying leads, driving outreach campaigns, and supporting the broader sales effort.

You will:

 - Own inbound lead management: Rapidly respond to and qualify inbound interest from GPs, lenders, and private market deal teams.
   

 - Execute outbound campaigns: Partner with Mid-Market and Enterprise AEs to design and execute outbound sequences targeting high-value accounts.
   

 - Set meetings that matter: Your success will be measured by qualified meetings set and pipeline created.
   

 - Support sales collateral creation: Collaborate with AEs and Marketing to refine messaging, tailor outreach, and build decks and 1-pagers.
   

 - Keep CRM immaculate: Ensure our pipeline is always accurate and up to date in HubSpot/Salesforce.
   

 - Learn the product and space deeply: Get fluent in private markets workflows and Arc’s differentiated AI offering. Your credibility with our buyers will be key.
   

 - Constantly iterate: Help refine the BDR playbook, report on conversion metrics, and suggest improvements across the funnel.
   






YOU MIGHT BE A GREAT FIT IF

You’re not just a lead generator—you’re a high-IQ operator who knows how to get in front of the right people and speak their language.

We’re looking for someone with:

 - BDR or sales experience in a fast-paced, outbound-heavy environment (tech, SaaS, fintech, or AI preferred)
   

 - Domain knowledge in private credit, private equity, or commercial banking — either through direct experience or deep exposure
   

 - Entrepreneurial hustle or a founder-like mentality — you work insanely hard, thrive in ambiguity, move fast, have a positive attitude and don't need hand-holding
   

 - Strong communication skills, both written and verbal — you know how to craft a tight outbound message and hold your own on calls
   

 - Tools fluency — you’re comfortable with CRM (Salesforce, HubSpot), sales engagement platforms (e.g. Outreach, Apollo), and LinkedIn Sales Navigator
   

 - Analytical mindset — you can qualify prospects rigorously and think commercially about the funnel
   

 - Based in NYC — we believe the best work is done together, in person at our beautiful corporate HQ in NYC. You’ll have flexibility to work remotely at least 1 day per week.






PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience selling to finance personas (CFOs, deal professionals)



 - Background in investment banking, private credit, private equity or fund services



 - Interest in AI, automation, and enterprise software
   






WHY F2

 - Join a fast-growing AI startup reshaping workflows in private markets and finance.



 - Be part of a small, high-impact team where your design decisions inf

source_jobs.title: AI Financial Modeling Extern
source_jobs.description:
AI FINANCIAL MODELING EXTERN — F2 AI

Location: San Francisco, CA / In-Person or Remote

Commitment: 5+ hours per week | 4 - 12+ weeks

Compensation: $50/hr




ABOUT F2

F2 is the AI platform for private markets investors — purpose-built to transform how institutional teams underwrite, diligence, and deploy capital. Our AI converts messy, unstructured deal materials into investment-grade insights in minutes, helping private credit funds, commercial banks, and private equity firms make faster, more confident decisions. Backed by leading investors, including NFX and Y Combinator, we’re scaling a world-class product and engineering team to build the future of vertical AI for finance.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are seeking 1–2 high-performing externs from top-tier Investment Banking or Private Equity backgrounds to help architect AI-native financial modeling on the F2 platform.



You will work directly with the EPD and Sales team in our SF office to translate institutional-grade financial modeling standards into agentic, automated workflows. This is a hands-on opportunity to help define how AI should build and reason through financial models.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Teach F2 agent best practices across financial models

 - Design and standardize financial modeling templates optimized for AI execution with first principles approach

 - Define formatting, structure, and best practices consistent with institutional modeling standards

 - Perform high-rigor QA on AI outputs to ensure investor-grade precision

 - Pressure-test edge cases and help identify failure modes in automated modeling workflows




YOU MIGHT BE A GREAT FIT IF

 - You have prior experience in Investment Banking, Private Credit, or Private Equity with heavy financial modeling exposure

 - You’ve built and audited complex 3-statement, LBO, or credit models from scratch

 - You have strong instincts for model hygiene, structure, and institutional formatting standards

 - You think critically about model logic and enjoy breaking / stress-testing systems

 - You’re excited about applying AI to transform financial workflows




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in investment banking, private credit or private equity.

 - Exposure to model auditing, QA processes, or financial modeling best-practice frameworks.




WHY F2?

 - Join a fast-growing AI startup reshaping workflows in private markets and finance.

 - Be part of a small, high-impact team where your work directly influences product and company trajectory.

 - Competitive compensation with equity upside and benefits.

 - Collaborative, mission-driven culture that values craftsmanship, curiosity, and rapid iteration.

source_jobs.title: Controller
source_jobs.description:
About F2

F2 is the AI platform for private markets investors — purpose-built to transform how institutional teams underwrite, diligence, and deploy capital. Our AI converts messy, unstructured deal materials into investment-grade insights in minutes, helping private credit funds, commercial banks, and private equity firms make faster, more confident decisions.

Backed by leading investors, including NFX and Y Combinator, we're scaling a world-class product and engineering team to build the future of vertical AI for finance. We are scaling quickly across our offices in New York, San Francisco, and London.



Role Overview

We're hiring our first in-house Controller to own accounting and build the financial backbone of F2. Partnering with our outsourced accounting team, you'll run a clean, timely close and take full ownership of our accounting systems, controls, and reporting as we scale.

This is an operator's seat, not a caretaker's. You'll design a scalable accounting and controls process from the ground up — leveraging AI and modern tooling to keep the function lean as we grow — while also supporting FP&A and budgeting. It's the right role for someone who has built accounting infrastructure in a high-growth startup, is energized by AI-native finance tools, and wants a clear path to grow into an accounting and finance leadership role.



What You'll Do

 - Own the month-end and quarter-end close in partnership with our outsourced accounting team — general ledger, journal entries, accruals, and reconciliations — delivering clean, final numbers on a predictable calendar.

 - Own our accounting systems and processes end-to-end, including our ERP (Rillet), expense management (Ramp), and payroll tooling.

 - Own and maintain the chart of accounts, and build the processes for expense management and record-keeping that keep it clean.

 - Ensure transactions are coded correctly all the way through the financials — from expense capture in Ramp to the general ledger.

 - Design accounting and control processes that scale with headcount and volume, leveraging AI and automation to streamline close, reconciliations, and reporting.

 - Own revenue recognition, AP/AR, payroll, and expense management, and establish financial controls appropriate to our stage.

 - Own tax coordination, statutory filings, and audit readiness so every future raise and eventual audit is clean.

 - Support FP&A and budgeting — budget-vs-actuals, departmental spend tracking, and clean historical actuals that feed planning.

 - Track cash and burn as the source of truth on our runway, and produce the financials and KPIs used in leadership reporting.

 - Direct and quality-check the outsourced accounting team, deciding over time what stays outsourced versus comes in-house as we grow.



You Might Be a Great Fit If

 - You have 4+ years of progressive accounting experience, including ownership of a monthly close.

 - You are a CPA.

 - You've worked inside a venture-backed startup or SaaS company and understand its pace and priorities.

 - You're comfortable being the sole in-house accountant while directing an outsourced team — you operate independently and set your own standards.

 - You have an automation-first mindset and are genuinely excited to use AI and modern tools to build lean, scalable processes.

 - You have a strong command of GAAP and financial controls, plus solid Excel and financial-modeling fundamentals.

 - You communicate clearly and can hand off clean numbers and explain them to non-finance leaders.

 - You move quickly, operate with precision, and thrive in ambiguity.

 - You are energized by AI, private markets, and the chance to help build a category-defining company.

Preferred Qualifications

 - AI-native FinOps experience — building and running finance operations on AI-first tooling (a big plus).

 - Experience with a modern / AI-native ERP (we use Rillet) and spend tools like Ramp.

 - Experience managing or supporting FP&A for early stag

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
F2 STAFF SOFTWARE ENGINEER, INFRASTRUCTURE

Location: San Francisco Employment Type: Full time Location Type: Hybrid Department: Engineering, Product, Design (EPD)

Compensation: $200K – $300K • Offers Equity

The total rewards package at F2 includes base salary, equity (stock options), and benefits.

--

The Role:

We are seeking a hands-on Infrastructure Tech Lead who thrives in building from 0‑1 and driving execution, directly contributing production-level systems while shaping our core technical foundation. You will collaborate closely with the founding team to architect, deploy, and scale the cloud infrastructure powering F2's AI-driven enterprise analytics platform.

What you'll do:

 - Hands-on platform building. Architect and implement foundational cloud infrastructure from scratch: compute, networking, CI/CD, and observability. Actively writing infrastructure-as-code and shipping production systems, not just diagrams.

 - Own the infrastructure architecture. Define and execute the technical vision and multi-year roadmap for our AWS-based platform (ECS/Fargate, containerized Python and Node services), ensuring rapid iteration while scaling reliably with enterprise demand.

 - Run durable, AI-heavy workloads. Operate and scale our workflow orchestration layer (Temporal), streaming pipelines, vector search infrastructure, and high-throughput LLM inference paths; balancing latency, throughput, and cost.

 - Design for security and compliance. Build infrastructure that meets SOC 2 requirements from day one: multi-tenant isolation, secrets management, least-privilege IAM, audit logging, and encrypted data flows across storage, database, and AI providers.

 - Establish operational excellence. Set and uphold standards for IaC, deployment pipelines, incident response, SLOs, and on-call practices; mentor engineers to foster a culture of reliable, observable, cost-efficient systems.

 - Cross-functional collaboration. Partner with product, backend, frontend, and enterprise customers to translate requirements (data residency, uptime guarantees, deployment models_ into pragmatic infrastructure solutions.

What you bring:

 - Hands-on technical leadership. 7+ years building and scaling mission-critical cloud infrastructure on AWS and/or GCP, with demonstrated experience architecting platforms from the ground up.

 - Deep container and orchestration expertise. Production experience with ECS/Fargate, Kubernetes, or equivalent: including service networking, autoscaling, zero-downtime deploys, and multi-environment release strategies.

 - Infrastructure-as-code fluency. Strong command of Terraform, Pulumi, or CloudFormation, plus CI/CD pipeline design (GitHub Actions or similar) and GitOps workflows.

 - Data and AI infrastructure experience. Familiarity operating Postgres at scale (RDS, Supabase, or self-managed), Redis, message/workflow systems (Temporal, SQS, Kafka), and ideally vector databases or LLM serving infrastructure.

 - Security-first mindset. Practical experience with SOC 2 (or similar) compliance programs, IAM design, VPC architecture, secrets management, and multi-tenant data isolation.

 - Execution-driven mindset. You thrive in environments where you own systems end-to-end, iterate rapidly, and deliver production-ready solutions; and you measure success in uptime, latency, and dollars saved.

 - Passionate builder who enjoys solving complex distributed-systems challenges and iterating toward clear, impactful goals.

source_jobs.title: Head of Talent
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT F2

F2 is the AI platform for private markets investors — purpose-built to transform how institutional teams underwrite, diligence, and deploy capital. Our AI converts messy, unstructured deal materials into investment-grade insights in minutes, helping private credit funds, commercial banks, and private equity firms make faster, more confident decisions.

Backed by leading investors, including NFX and Y Combinator, we’re scaling a world-class product and engineering team to build the future of vertical AI for finance. We are scaling quickly across our offices in New York, San Francisco, and London.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We're hiring a Head of Talent to build and own the full recruiting function at F2 — from strategy to close. We're doubling our headcount over the next two quarters, and this role is critical to making that happen. You'll partner directly with Finance and senior leadership to build hiring plans, set comp bands, and bring the caliber of people who will define what F2 becomes.

As our first dedicated talent leader, you'll build the function from the ground up — with real ownership and real impact. This is the right role for someone who has built hiring infrastructure from scratch in a high-growth startup environment, knows how to recruit without relying on big-company brand leverage, and can operate as both a strategic talent partner and a hands-on full-cycle recruiter.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own full-cycle recruiting across functions, with an emphasis on engineering, product, AI, deployment, and GTM roles.

 - Build the recruiting engine from the ground up: sourcing strategy, pipeline architecture, interview process, scorecards, candidate experience, offer process, and close strategy.

 - Partner with leadership and Finance on headcount planning, role prioritization, comp bands, recruiting budget, and agency ROI.

 - Bring a sales mindset to candidate engagement, including outbound messaging, narrative-building, objection handling, and close strategy.

 - Build strong pipelines in competitive talent markets, especially NYC and SF engineering, AI, product, and GTM ecosystems.

 - Create lightweight but rigorous recruiting systems, including ATS hygiene, reporting, funnel metrics, source quality, and hiring manager accountability.

 - Act as a talent brand ambassador for F2, helping candidates understand the size of the opportunity, the quality of the team, and the urgency of the market.

 - Help the company scale thoughtfully across New York, San Francisco, and London while keeping talent density high.




YOU MIGHT BE A GREAT FIT IF

 - You have 6+ years of recruiting experience, including meaningful time in an early-stage or high-growth startup environment.

 - You have built a hiring function or recruiting engine from scratch, not just operated inside an established recruiting machine.

 - You have hired across technical and go-to-market roles, ideally including software engineering, product, AI, sales, and customer-facing roles.

 - You understand what it takes to recruit in low-brand-leverage environments where candidate selling, precision, persistence, and market mapping matter.

 - You are fluent in the NYC and SF talent markets and understand how competitive engineering and AI recruiting have become.

 - You are highly commercial: you can build a candidate narrative, sell the company, navigate objections, and close top talent.

 - You are analytical and comfortable with headcount plans, recruiting funnels, comp calibration, and basic recruiting finance.

 - You move quickly, operate with precision, and thrive in ambiguity.

 - You are energized by AI, private markets, and the chance to help build a category-defining company.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience scaling a startup from pre-seed, seed, or Series A through a major growth phase.

 - Experience hiring for AI, fintech, B2B SaaS, infrastructure, or vertical software companies.

 - Strong track record recruiting in both New York and San

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Role:

We're looking for a Forward Deployed Engineer to be the bridge between F2's platform and the real-world complexity of our most important enterprise clients. You'll embed with strategic Private Credit customers, get deep on their data, workflows, and infrastructure, and build custom solutions that makes F2 indispensable to how they operate. This is a hands-on engineering role: you write production code, you ship it and you see your work create value in days, not quarters.

You'll spend the majority of your time in the field with customers, then bring what you learn back to our core engineering and product teams to make the platform better for everyone. It's the ideal role for an engineer who wants to combine deep technical work with direct customer impact, and who's excited to learn a modern AI stack end-to-end.



What you'll do:

 - Embed with strategic accounts. Sit with our top enterprise clients to deeply understand their domain, data sources, security requirements, and analyst workflows and then translate those into concrete technical solutions.

 - Build custom integrations and solutions. Write production code on top of F2's platform: connectors to customer data rooms and document management systems, ingestion pipelines, workflow automations, and domain-specific features tailored to each client's needs.

 - Own the technical relationship. Act as the trusted technical partner for the customer's team throughout the engagement; from initial deployment and configuration through production rollout and optimization.

 - Prototype, validate, ship. Move fast: build a prototype, put it in front of the customer, iterate on real feedback, and get it into production through our CI/CD pipelines while meeting our engineering standards.

 - Generalize back into the platform. Return from engagements and work with core engineering and product to turn one-off customer solutions into reusable abstractions and platform capabilities that scale across our entire customer base.

 - Be the voice of the customer. Bring hard-won field insights back to the roadmap, championing the features and integrations that solve real enterprise problems.

 - Work alongside core engineering. Participate in shared code review, architecture discussions, you're a full member of the engineering org, not a siloed services team.



What you bring:

 - Hands-on full-stack or backend engineering. 3+ years shipping production software, ideally with Python and/or TypeScript. You're comfortable owning a feature end-to-end across an unfamiliar codebase.

 - Customer-facing instincts. You communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, and you enjoy being in the room with customers translating ambiguous requirements into working software.

 - Pragmatic and fast. You prototype, validate, and ship in days and weeks, not months. You'd rather get something real in front of a customer than perfect a diagram.

 - Comfort with cloud and infra fundamentals. Working familiarity with AWS, containers, CI/CD, and Postgres/SQL. Experience with Temporal, Redis, and LLM tooling is a strong plus.

 - Integration mindset. Experience building connectors, APIs, or ETL/data pipelines against messy real-world data sources and enterprise systems.

 - Security awareness. You understand why enterprise clients care about tenant isolation, least-privilege access, and data handling, and you build with those constraints in mind.

 - Builder's curiosity. You enjoy learning a new domain (private markets / finance) and a full AI application stack, and you thrive in a 0→1 environment where you own outcomes.

notes: F2 is a vertical AI platform for private markets investors, with a strong focus on product and engineering. The company is scaling quickly and has a mix of technical and non-technical roles.
